---
title: "Publications"
page-layout: full
---

# Publications

This page lists peer-reviewed publications, theses, archived work, preprints, and public manuscripts.

---

## Peer-reviewed Publications

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Bundle-Theoretic Structure and Computational Modeling of Quantum Contextuality

**Sahil Imtiyaz**, Emanuela Merelli, and Serafim Rodrigues.  
In *Quantum AI and NLP: First International Conference, QNLPAI 2025, Bloomington, IN, USA, August 6–9, 2025, Proceedings*, Communications in Computer and Information Science, vol. 2659, Springer, Cham, 2026, pp. 274–301.  
DOI: [`10.1007/978-3-032-13883-5_15`](https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-032-13883-5_15)

**Contribution.**  
Develops a bundle-theoretic and computational framework for quantum contextuality and non-locality, treating contextual behaviour as a local-to-global obstruction encoded by geometric, topological, and symmetry-based structure. The work also develops a topology-driven model of computation for contextual semantics machines, linking quantum foundations, contextuality, quantum cognition, and computational modelling through a common structural language.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Understanding Entanglement Through the Lens of Quantifiable Algebraic Structures: Application to Bird Navigation

**Sahil Imtiyaz** and Serafim Rodrigues.  
*IEEE Transactions on Molecular, Biological, and Multi-Scale Communications*, vol. 12, 2026, pp. 279–297.  
DOI: [`10.1109/TMBMC.2025.3650156`](https://doi.org/10.1109/TMBMC.2025.3650156)

**Contribution.**  
Introduces an algebraic and structural quantum-compass perspective for multisensory integration in avian navigation. The paper treats cue integration as a global-consistency problem and proposes loop-based observables for detecting contextual conflict, remapping, and non-local integration effects in evolving biological scaffolds.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Four-Cycles Delay-Efficient RPL-Based Routing Approach for IoT Applications

Manar Al Bataineh, Tasneem Al Mallah, Wail Mardini, **Sahil Imtiyaz**, Yaser Khamayseh, and Muneer Bani Yassein.  
In *2021 12th International Conference on Information and Communication Systems (ICICS)*, Valencia, Spain / virtual, 24–26 May 2021.  
DOI: [`10.1109/ICICS52457.2021.9464550`](https://doi.org/10.1109/ICICS52457.2021.9464550)  
Conference programme: [ICICS 2021, Paper ID 119](https://www.just.edu.jo/icics/icics2021/program.html)

**Contribution.**  
Proposes a four-cycle-based enhancement of RPL routing for IoT and low-power network settings, with emphasis on delay-efficient communication and routing behaviour in constrained networked infrastructures.

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Theses and Archived Work

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Contextual Semantics Machinery

**Sahil Imtiyaz**.  
Doctoral thesis, PhD in Computer Science and Mathematics, University of Camerino, 2023.  
Supervisor: [Emanuela Merelli](https://www.emanuelamerelli.eu/).  
Research environment: [BioShape & Data Science Lab](https://www.emanuelamerelli.eu/bigdata/), University of Camerino.  
Scientific programme/influence: [Mario Rasetti](https://www.polito.it/en/staff?p=mario.rasetti), Topological Field Theory of Data, and topology-driven methods for complex systems.  
Institutional repository handle: `11581/484452`.  
Link: [UNICAM repository](https://pubblicazioni.unicam.it/handle/11581/484452)

**Research context and funding.**  
Developed within the BioShape & Data Science Lab research environment, connected to the Topological Field Theory of Data programme and the EU FP7/FET TOPDRIM project, *Topology Driven Methods for Complex Systems*, grant agreement `318121`.

Links: [TOPDRIM website](https://www.topdrim.eu/) · [CORDIS TOPDRIM record](https://cordis.europa.eu/project/id/318121)

**Contribution.**  
Develops contextual semantics machinery within the broader programme of Topological Field Theory of Data. The thesis studies how local contextual structures, topological data, and quantum contextuality can be organized into a mathematical framework for complex systems, computation, and topology-driven modelling.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Quantum Adiabatic Evolution for Global Optimization in Big Data

**Sahil Imtiyaz**.  
MS thesis / archived early research work, International Islamic University Islamabad, 2018.  
Supervisor: [Dr. Jamal Abdul Nasir / Jamal Nasir](https://research.universityofgalway.ie/en/persons/jamal-nasir/).  
Additional profile: [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=V86JSRwAAAAJ).  
arXiv: [`1805.11479`](https://arxiv.org/abs/1805.11479)

**Contribution.**  
Explores quantum adiabatic evolution and optimization ideas for large and complex data spaces, forming an early computational foundation for later work on structure-sensitive modelling, quantum computation, and complex data.

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Preprints and Public Manuscripts

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Constraint Semantics for Multi-Level Organisation

**Sahil Imtiyaz**, Serafim Rodrigues, and Mathieu Desroches.  
bioRxiv preprint / manuscript, 2026.  
DOI: [`10.64898/2026.02.27.708558`](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.64898/2026.02.27.708558v1)

**Contribution.**  
Develops a structural semantics for multi-level biological organisation, modelling biological systems through admissible configurations, active interfaces, histories, and global realisability. The work provides a mathematical language for distinguishing local admissibility, coherent multi-level organisation, and structural reorganisation.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### The Gauge-Theoretic Structure of Non-Locality and Contextuality

**Sahil Imtiyaz** and Serafim Rodrigues.  
Public repository manuscript / preprint, BCAM BIRD, 2025.  
Handle: `20.500.11824/2095`.  
Link: [BCAM BIRD record](https://bird.bcamath.org/handle/20.500.11824/2095)

**Contribution.**  
Studies quantum non-locality and contextuality through gauge-theoretic, bundle-theoretic, topological, and algebraic structures. The work investigates Bell–Kochen–Specker models, local-to-global obstruction, symmetry principles, loop/interface holonomy, and the geometric foundations of quantum contextuality.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### Contextual Semantics Machinery

**Sahil Imtiyaz** and Emanuela Merelli.  
Public repository manuscript / working paper, BCAM BIRD, 2024.  
Handle: `20.500.11824/1889`.  
Link: [BCAM BIRD record](https://bird.bcamath.org/handle/20.500.11824/1889)

**Contribution.**  
Presents a contextual-semantics framework connecting Topological Field Theory of Data, quantum contextuality, and computation. The work serves as a bridge between local-global obstruction, topology-driven modelling, and computational semantics.

:::
:::
:::

:::

---

## Related Pages

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Research](research.qmd)

Research areas, methods, and current directions.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [Projects](projects.qmd)

Ongoing projects, research context, and funding environment.

:::
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.card .h-100}
::: {.card-body}

### [CV](cv.qmd)

Academic CV and professional profile.

:::
:::
:::

:::
